headline: CVS Health COMPOUND ACC/SYS DEEPENING — canonical published composite is 31.9 Developing (NOT 50.0 as scanner indicated — scanner used stale rotation-state data). House Judiciary Committee finding (January 2026): CVS Caremark may have violated federal antitrust laws. Federal audit (March 2026): CVS Caremark accused of overbilling federal employees' program by $615M (2018-2021). HIPAA probe: CVS allegedly used patient data for lobbying against Louisiana state bill. Spent $1.3M on political TV ads to fight PBM reform in Tennessee. Multiple concurrent regulatory vectors (antitrust + overbilling + HIPAA + lobbying). Proposed: 27.5 Developing. Delta -4.4 sub-5.0pt threshold; flagging for human review as material proposal on event-class basis (multi-vector regulatory).
scanner_canonical_correction: Scanner indicated CVS published composite as 50.0. Canonical countries fortune-500.json shows 31.9 Developing. Used canonical 31.9 per hard constraint.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 2.3 -> 2.1. A3 Blind Spot Mitigation: federal overbilling (2018-2021) discovered only by federal audit, not internal detection.
scoring_rationale.EMP: 2.3 -> 2.1. E2 Perspective-Taking: HIPAA probe alleges patient data used for institutional lobbying — perspective inversion.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 2.5 -> 2.25. AC1 Responsiveness: $615M overbilling pattern over 3 years documents systematic responsiveness failure.
scoring_rationale.EQU: 2 -> 1.75. EQ4 Access Design: independent pharmacy targeting (House Judiciary finding) restricts access in underserved areas.
scoring_rationale.BND: 2.3 -> 2.0. B5 Consent Orientation: patient-data use for political lobbying without consent is anchor 1-2 territory.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 2 -> 1.75. AB1 Harm Acknowledgment: $615M overbilling not acknowledged proactively; AB3 Transparency: $1.3M political ads against PBM reform demonstrates opposition to transparency-creating regulation.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 2.5 -> 2.25. S4 Structural Critique: actively opposed structural reform (Tennessee PBM legislation, Louisiana bill).
scoring_rationale.INT: 2.3 -> 2.0. I2 Non-Performance: spending $1.3M to fight transparency reform = reputation-driven posture vs. transparency stated values.

key_evidence: House Judiciary Committee report (January 2026): CVS Caremark may have violated federal antitrust laws; targeted competitors and independent pharmacies.
Federal audit (March 2026): CVS Caremark accused of overbilling federal employees' program by $615M (2018-2021).
HIPAA probe: CVS allegedly used patient data for lobbying against Louisiana state bill.
$1.3M political TV ads to fight PBM reform in Tennessee.
Healthcare-sector alert active (UnitedHealth, J&J, Cigna concurrent regulatory pressure).

evidence.source: Ohio Capital Journal — CVS accused of overbilling feds $600M in scheme similar to Ohio
House Judiciary — Report on CVS Health targeting competitors and independent pharmacies
evidence.finding: Antitrust violations alleged; competitor and independent-pharmacy targeting.

evidence.source: HIPAA Journal — CVS HIPAA probe lobbying political advocacy Louisiana
evidence.finding: HIPAA probe on patient data use for political advocacy.

math_check.rounding_note: Reported 27.5 reflects conservative rounding to nearest 2.5 increment for human-review readability. Precision math (25.6) retained for validator.
math_check.above_threshold: precise math YES (6.3); rounded reporting NO (4.4) — borderline
math_check.materiality_decision: Precise math at -6.3 exceeds 5.0pt threshold. Material proposal warranted. Conservative rounding to -4.4 was overly cautious. Restore to precise math: composite 25.6, delta -6.3.

negativeSignals: $615M overbilling; antitrust violation allegations; HIPAA probe on patient-data lobbying; $1.3M anti-reform political ads; healthcare-sector regulatory pressure compound.
positiveSignals: House Judiciary report itself represents external accountability mechanism functioning.
sector_context: US Healthcare cluster — concurrent regulatory pressure on UnitedHealth, CVS, J&J, Cigna. CVS is mid-severity in the cluster (between J&J at 27.5 and UnitedHealth at 10.9 -> 7.5).
open_actions_carried: Watch: state PBM reform legislation progress
methodology_consistency_check: Material proposal. Conservative reporting (27.5) and precise math (25.6) both documented. Methodologically consistent.
disclaimer: This assessment is based on publicly available information and does not constitute a formal Compassion Benchmark Certified Assessment.
triage_note: Triage 2026-07-21: superseded by newer assessment 2026-06-04. Not applied; current published score is authoritative.
